page: Microsoft Azure D96nds_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Dndv6 server family. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor, this instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

The instance is equipped with 5669 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. As a network-optimized server, it is designed to handle demanding networking workloads alongside its substantial local storage capacity. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

Given its high resource density, the D96nds_v6 offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale deployments requiring high-capacity local storage and balanced memory-to-CPU ratios without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. This configuration is suitable for workloads such as relational databases, large-scale web serving, network-intensive applications, and general-purpose enterprise computing.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96nds_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dndv6 family, powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage-heavy operations, the instance includes 5669 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server is designed without GPU accelerators, prioritizing high-capacity general-purpose compute and network performance. The combination of dense local storage, high vCPU count, and network optimization makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, high-throughput network applications, large-scale web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
